content:
- Read current task summary and done-task summary.
- Resolved blockers: no active failing bug tasks; blocked tasks are config simplification and postgres role reconciliation because their story blockers are not fully complete.
- Unblocked failing tasks are: cert reload (low), ctl 09 three-etcd given (low), architecture 02 (medium), architecture 04 (medium), architecture 05 (medium), logging simplification (low).
- Read the medium-priority candidate task files in detail to choose the most logical next step instead of defaulting to list order.
- Architecture 05 currently looks like the best next task because it directly addresses duplicated HA givens and explicitly prepares the harness for the pending three-etcd given work, avoiding more copied topology trees before that work starts.
- Next: confirm the dependency/fit with ctl task 09, then write the chosen task path to .ralph/current_task.txt and stop.

- Read the three-etcd task and confirmed the relationship: adding that topology now would create another large copied given tree unless the fixture pipeline is cleaned up first.
- Selected `.ralph/tasks/story-general-architecture-improvement-finding/05-task-collapse-duplicated-ha-givens-into-a-typed-topology-fixture-pipeline.md` as the most logical next task.
- Reasoning: no active failing bugs exist; among unblocked failing tasks, this is medium priority and directly prepares the HA harness for upcoming multi-etcd work while reducing duplication before more topology variants are introduced.
- Wrote the chosen task path to `.ralph/current_task.txt`.
- Stopping here per the choose-task instructions.

- Read .ralph/current_task.txt and found active task: .ralph/tasks/story-general-architecture-improvement-finding/05-task-collapse-duplicated-ha-givens-into-a-typed-topology-fixture-pipeline.md
- progress_read returned no visible prior notes, so I am treating this as a fresh local context.
- Next I am reading the task file and checking repo status before deciding whether the task is still in TO BE VERIFIED design mode or ready for NOW EXECUTE.

- Read givens/world entry points and confirmed the current API is a raw string -> directory path -> full tree copy into run workspace.
- Diffed three_node_plain vs three_node_custom_roles: only compose NET_ADMIN capability and postgres role names differ, while TLS/secrets/common configs are fully duplicated.
- Next I am locating the topology/type definitions and the full support module surface so I can define a typed given/materialization ADT and then deliberately break callers in one pass.

- Completed the design-only pass for task 05 and intentionally stopped before execution/fixing compile errors.
- Replaced the raw given directory API with typed HA fixture ADTs in tests/ha/support/givens/mod.rs: HaGivenId, HaGivenDefinition, HaTopologyFixture, ThreeNodeTopologyFixture, PostgresRoleMapping, FixtureMaterialization, SharedFixtureEntry, RenderedFixtureFile, and template/render target enums.
- Reworked the harness types in tests/ha/support/world/mod.rs toward typed workspace/compose state: HarnessWorkspace, WorkspacePaths, ComposeStack, and changed HarnessShared::initialize to take HaGivenId and resolve a typed given definition.
- Updated the cucumber step entry point in tests/ha/support/steps/mod.rs to parse the feature string into HaGivenId before initializing the harness.
- Added an explicit execution plan plus NOW EXECUTE marker to the task file so the next pass should implement materialize_given_fixture, migrate all world helpers/callers to the new typed fields, collapse the duplicated fixture trees, and then run make check/lint/test/test-long and docs.
- The repo is intentionally left in a non-compiling intermediate state because the user instruction for the no-marker/TO BE VERIFIED branch said to change all types first, break types at once, mark NOW EXECUTE, and quit immediately.

- Confirmed active task 05 is in NOW EXECUTE mode.
- The worktree is already dirty in the expected HA support files plus Ralph bookkeeping files; I will treat those as in-progress state and avoid reverting unrelated changes.
- Next I am reading the modified givens/world/steps modules and the related tests/fixtures so I can complete the materialization pipeline and then run the required make targets.

- Ran make check to get the first execution-pass compiler surface.
- Confirmed the type design is still viable: the errors are unfinished field migrations plus the missing materialize_given_fixture implementation, not a broken ADT boundary.
- Next I am patching world/mod.rs to use the typed workspace/compose state consistently, implement fixture materialization/rendering, migrate steps/mod.rs to the new field paths, and collapse the duplicated fixture directories into a shared root.

- Patched the harness to use typed workspace/compose state consistently and added rendering/materialization helpers for compose/runtime/observer outputs.
- Added focused unit tests in tests/ha/support/world/mod.rs to materialize both plain and custom-role variants and verify the generated config differences plus copied shared assets.
- Collapsed fixture assets into tests/ha/givens/three_node_shared and deleted the tracked files under the old plain/custom_roles trees; next is another make check pass to catch the remaining compiler/test issues.

- Completed execution for task 05: typed given/materialization pipeline is wired, shared assets collapsed into three_node_shared, docs updated, and the task file is marked done/passes=true.
- Validation status: make check passed, make lint passed twice (including after the docs update), make test passed, make test-long passed with all 26 ultra-long HA scenarios green.
- Next I am doing final Ralph closeout only: task_switch.sh, git add/commit with the required task-finished message, and git push.
